member.name: T:PasswordHashing.Extensions.DependencyInjection
member.summary: Provides extension methods for registering password hashing services and configuring related options in the dependency injection container.

member.name: M:PasswordHashing.Extensions.DependencyInjection.AddPasswordHasher(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{PasswordHashing.Hasher.PasswordHashOptions})
member.summary: Registers the service and allows configuration of .
member.param: An optional delegate to configure . If not provided, default secure values will be used.
member.remarks: This method applies default hashing settings, which can be overridden by the provided delegate.

member.name: M:PasswordHashing.Extensions.DependencyInjection.AddPasswordHasher(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Registers the service using default configuration values.

member.name: T:PasswordHashing.Hasher.PasswordHashOptions
member.summary: Represents configuration options for password hashing. These settings control the behavior and security characteristics of the underlying hashing algorithm (e.g., Argon2).

member.name: P:PasswordHashing.Hasher.PasswordHashOptions.SaltSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the randomly generated salt in bytes. A larger salt increases resistance against precomputed attacks (e.g., rainbow tables).

member.name: P:PasswordHashing.Hasher.PasswordHashOptions.HashSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the generated hash in bytes. Larger hashes may provide better security but increase storage size.

member.name: P:PasswordHashing.Hasher.PasswordHashOptions.Iterations
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of iterations (time cost) used during hashing. Increasing this value makes hashing slower, improving resistance to brute-force attacks.

member.name: P:PasswordHashing.Hasher.PasswordHashOptions.MemorySizeInBytes
member.summary: Gets or sets the amount of memory (in bytes) used by the hashing algorithm. Higher values increase resistance to GPU/ASIC attacks but require more system resources.

member.name: P:PasswordHashing.Hasher.PasswordHashOptions.DegreeOfParallelism
member.summary: Gets or sets the degree of parallelism (number of threads) used during hashing. Higher values can improve performance on multi-core systems.

member.name: T:IPasswordHasher
member.summary: Provides functionality for securely hashing passwords and verifying them using a strong one-way hashing algorithm (e.g., Argon2).

member.name: M:IPasswordHasher.HashPassword(System.String)
member.summary: Hashes the specified plain-text password using a secure algorithm and returns a formatted string containing the hash and all required parameters (e.g., salt, iterations).
member.returns: A hashed representation of the password, typically encoded in a standard format (e.g., PHC string format) that includes the algorithm parameters.
member.exception: Thrown when is empty or invalid.

member.name: M:IPasswordHasher.VerifyPassword(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies whether the provided plain-text password matches the specified hashed password.
member.param: The stored hashed password (including algorithm parameters and salt).
member.returns: true if the password matches the hash; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This method should perform comparisons in constant time to mitigate timing attacks.
